Pass it as the `world_model` argument of the `python` tool call, alongside `code`. That argument is the one that carries forward, so send it on every call, even when you reply with a tool call and no assistant text.

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
The selection moved! Looking at the image, the dark gray ring is now around the second green object (at col 24), and the first green object's cell now shows a gray marker. This confirms:
1. Clicking a green object moves the selection to it
2. The previously selected object gets a gray marker (g color)
So the pattern is: click green objects to select them one by one, and the previous selection gets marked. The goal is probably to select all 5 green objects. Let me continue clicking the remaining green objects.
Green object centers: (19,18), (19,24), (19,36), (25,42), (37,42)
- Already selected: (19,18) → now marked gray
- Currently selected: (19,24)
- Remaining: (19,36), (25,42), (37,42)
Let me click all remaining green objects in sequence.
